THE RISE REPORT
YOUR WEEKLY READ ON THE REFEREE WORLD
Week of June 8, 2026
Rise officials at the 2026 MLS NEXT Cup, Zions Bank Stadium, Salt Lake City.  Photo: Rise Officials
SEASON FINALE

The Season's Biggest Referee Stage: MLS NEXT Cup

The 2025–26 MLS NEXT season closed at the 2026 MLS NEXT Cup presented by Allstate, May 23–31 at Zions Bank Stadium in Salt Lake City — and it doubled as one of the strongest referee development environments in youth soccer. 210 referees worked the event, 115 of them Regional Referees (about 54%) — a concentration no US youth competition outside the Generation adidas Cup can match. Among them were 31 National Referee Candidates, officials who have met every requirement for National status and are now chasing the evaluations and exposure to advance — the threshold for PRO2 consideration.

What set the week apart was who was watching and teaching: US Soccer–funded National Referee Coaches, Macron-sponsored mentors drawn from active PRO staff, the Rise Family coaching group, and national leadership on site — including Kari Seitz, US Soccer Vice President and National Soccer Hall of Famer, who spoke to the group. To everyone who represented Rise in Utah: this is what the climb looks like.

A Rise crew at the MLS NEXT Cup U19 Academy Champions wall.  Photo: Rise Officials
COACHING, MENTORSHIP & LEADERSHIP ON SITE

US Soccer National Referee Coaches: Thomas Bobadilla · Steve Montanino · Richard Heron · Paul Scott

Rise / Macron-Sponsored Mentors (active PRO staff & professional officials): Frank Anderson · Victor Rivas · Nick Uranga · Mark Kadlecik

Rise Family Coaches: Allen Chapman · TJ Zablocki · Robert Sibiga

US Soccer Leadership: Kari Seitz (VP, US Soccer & Hall of Famer) · Nathan Magill (Director, Refereeing Education & Development) · Jeff Solis (National Referee identification & selection)

US Soccer United Officials Group: 12 referees with funded travel, supported by 3 National Referee Coaches.

📰 Around the World

As our season ends, the sport's biggest one begins — in our backyard. FIFA has named the largest officiating contingent in history for World Cup 2026: 52 referees, 88 assistant referees, and 30 VARs for 104 matches across the US, Canada and Mexico. Fifteen referees come from UEFA, among them Poland's Szymon Marciniak (the 2022 final referee) and England's Anthony Taylor and Michael Oliver. The tournament kicks off June 11 — required viewing for every official looking to study the very top of the craft.

📖 The Laws Desk

The new 2026/27 Laws of the Game take effect July 1, and the World Cup enforces the headline ones from Day 1. Three to start drilling now:

  • The countdown expands. Beyond the keeper's eight seconds, a five-second visual countdown now applies to throw-ins and goal kicks that are deliberately delayed — miss it and the restart goes to the opponent.
  • Leaving the field to protest = red card. Important: this is for each competition to adopt and decide — it is not a referee's decision — and it's designed for major events like the World Cup and Euros. Where a competition puts it in force, officials who incite it can be carded too.
  • Smarter advantage. Play advantage on a DOGSO foul and a goal is scored → no caution, since the offense didn't stop the goal.

⭐ Rise Spotlight

Istiaq Jamal (pictured with his crew) was the referee of the event.  Photo: Rise Officials

This week we're recognizing Istiaq Jamal, the referee of the event at the 2026 MLS NEXT Cup. Earning the marquee assignment at a tournament stacked with National Referee Candidates and PRO-level mentors is one of the highest honors an official can take from the week — and a clear marker of the trust he's earned. Congratulations, Istiaq. This is exactly what the climb looks like.

📅 The Week Ahead

World Cup kicks off June 11. Watch how the world's best handle the new countdown and the expanded VAR — which can now review incorrect second yellow cards and wrongly-awarded corners for the first time. Pick one crew and study their positioning all tournament. The offseason is when good officials get better.

Keep rising.
— The Rise Officials Team
